generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HvacActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator integrated within BIM environment enabling direct control signals to Building Control System (BCS) for HVAC systems in LEED Platinum academic building. Supports metadata extraction for device IDs/endpoint addresses."
]

instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator integrated within BIM environment enabling direct control signals to Building Control System (BCS) for lighting systems in LEED Platinum academic building. Supports metadata extraction for device IDs/endpoint addresses."
]

instance EnergyManagementActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator integrated within BIM environment enabling direct control signals to Building Control System (BCS) for energy management components in LEED Platinum academic building. Supports metadata extraction for device IDs/endpoint addresses."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance IndoorTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy-aware temperature sensor integrated into smart building infrastructure for real-time indoor environmental monitoring in LEED Platinum academic building."
    DTDFVocab:producedData IndoorTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance IndoorHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy-aware humidity sensor integrated into smart building infrastructure for real-time indoor environmental monitoring in LEED Platinum academic building."
    DTDFVocab:producedData IndoorHumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motion/presence sensor integrated into smart building infrastructure for real-time occupancy detection in LEED Platinum academic building."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy metering sensor integrated into smart building infrastructure for real-time energy consumption analytics in LEED Platinum academic building."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance AirQualitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor measuring indoor air quality parameters (CO2, VOCs) integrated into smart building infrastructure for real-time monitoring in LEED Platinum academic building."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualityDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance IndoorTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IndoorTemperatureSensor
]

instance IndoorHumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IndoorHumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance AirQualityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirQualitySensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance Bim3dModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically rich object-based 3D model encapsulating geometric, spatial, and functional characteristics of building components and their interrelationships, structured as IFC or proprietary formats."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance DesignTimeBimSchematics : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static design representations from BIM models serving as foundational input for Digital Twin deployment, including clash detection and performance simulation capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Live sensor feeds and IoT telemetry synchronized via Eclipse Hono and Ditto for continuous synchronization between physical and digital representations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData IndoorTemperatureDataTransmitted, IndoorHumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted, AirQualityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance IndoorEnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated indoor environmental parameters (temperature, humidity, occupancy, air quality) for real-time monitoring and analytics."
    DTDFVocab:fromData IndoorTemperatureDataTransmitted, IndoorHumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, AirQualityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance EnergyPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Energy consumption analytics and performance metrics derived from real-time sensor data and BIM model integration."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OptimizationEngineEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight M2M service for IoT data acquisition enabling real-time synchronization of sensor feeds between physical and digital representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataSynchronizationService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin framework for managing device representations and state synchronization, ensuring continuous alignment between physical assets and digital models."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceStateManagementService, RealTimeDataSynchronizationService
]

instance SimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation engine (e.g., thermal, airflow) integrated via MDE pipelines for high-fidelity building performance evaluation."
    DTDFVocab:enables BuildingPerformanceSimulationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization library (e.g., itwin.js) enabling immersive interfaces for exploring Digital Twin data and building performance metrics."
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService, DecisionSupportDashboardService
]

instance OptimizationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Adaptive control algorithms for dynamic adjustment of HVAC and lighting systems to minimize energy consumption while maintaining occupant comfort."
    DTDFVocab:enables EnergyOptimizationService, OccupantComfortOptimizationService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Domain-specific languages (DSLs) and meta-modeling tools enabling automated extraction of structured representations from BIM models for Digital Twin deployment."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelDeploymentService, ArtifactGenerationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeDataSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous synchronization service ensuring real-time alignment between physical building infrastructure and Digital Twin representations."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeSystemStateInsight
]

instance DeviceStateManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing device representations and state synchronization for all integrated IoT components in the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceStatusInsight
]

instance BuildingPerformanceSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "High-fidelity simulation service evaluating building performance under varying conditions using physics-based models."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceTrendInsight, ScenarioAnalysisInsight
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D visualization service enabling exploration of Digital Twin data and building performance metrics."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizedSystemStateInsight
]

instance DecisionSupportDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Customizable dashboard service providing role-specific insights for facility managers and building control engineers."
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomAnalyticsInsight
]

instance EnergyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic optimization service adjusting HVAC and lighting systems to minimize energy consumption while maintaining comfort."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyRecommendationAction
]

instance OccupantComfortOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring occupant comfort through adaptive control of environmental parameters based on real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComfortLevelInsight, AdaptiveControlAction
]

instance ModelDeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated service generating deployable Digital Twin artifacts from BIM models using model-driven engineering principles."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentValidationInsight
]

instance ArtifactGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service creating reproducible Digital Twin setups from BIM models with automated validation of correctness."
    DTDFVocab:provides ArtifactValidationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeSystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Facility managers observe operational states (sensor/actuator status, control logic execution) via immersive visualization interfaces."
]

instance DeviceStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current status and operational metrics of all integrated IoT devices in the Digital Twin system."
]

instance PerformanceTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical and real-time performance trends of building systems derived from simulation and operational data."
]

instance ScenarioAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Evaluated building performance under hypothetical scenarios using high-fidelity simulation models."
]

instance VisualizedSystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D visualization of current building state including environmental parameters, occupancy, and system performance."
]

instance CustomAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Domain-specific analytics (predictive maintenance, energy optimization) developed as plugins for the Digital Twin system."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendations for adjusting HVAC settings based on occupancy patterns and energy consumption data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AdaptiveControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of building systems (HVAC, lighting) to maintain optimal occupant comfort while minimizing energy use."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DeploymentValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results ensuring high fidelity between BIM models and deployed Digital Twin components."
]

instance ArtifactValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Automated validation of generated Digital Twin artifacts to ensure correctness and reproducibility."
]

instance ComfortLevelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time assessment of occupant comfort based on environmental parameters and occupancy data."
]

// C1: Environment
instance LeedPlatinumAcademicBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Research-oriented Digital Twin environment for a LEED Platinum certified academic building serving as a living lab for indoor environmental monitoring and operational optimization."
    base:contains Bim3dModel, DesignTimeBimSchematics, IndoorTemperatureSensor,
                 IndoorHumiditySensor, OccupancySensor, EnergyConsumptionSensor,
                 AirQualitySensor, HvacActuator, LightingActuator,
                 EnergyManagementActuator
]

instance LeedPlatinumAcademicBuildingEnvironmentComponent : DTDFVocab:DTComponent [
    base:isContainedIn LeedPlatinumAcademicBuildingEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin enables **control flows** from the virtual model to physical infrastructure, allowing Facility Managers to monitor and operate the physical building via the Digital Twin interface. The system supports **bidirectional communication** between the Digital Twin and physical components, though specific command types (e.g., HVAC setpoints), message formats (e.g., JSON), or validation/authentication mechanisms (e.g., RBAC, digital signatures) are not described. Feedback systems are implied through the **real-time synchronization** between the Digital Twin and physical systems, but conflict resolution or command execution confirmation protocols are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling near-instantaneous bidirectional updates. While the document does not specify exact synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms, or event-driven triggers), the emphasis on **real-time** implies sub-second latency requirements for critical operational services (e.g., building control systems). Temporal granularity is implicitly tied to the **dynamic operational needs** of the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain, where adjustments (e.g., sensor data, environmental parameters) must align with the **runtime configuration management** framework. The system supports **modular service integration**, allowing individual components (e.g., analytics, monitoring) to operate at varying time scales, but the overarching constraint is the **low-latency, high-fidelity synchronization** demanded by facility managers for real-time monitoring and control. No explicit mention of hard latency thresholds (e.g., <100ms) is provided, but the context implies **millisecond-to-second-scale** responsiveness for interactive use cases (e.g., immersive visualization)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is structured as a **service-oriented framework** with a **central DT Framework hub** acting as a **mediation layer** for bidirectional data exchange between enriched BIM models and operational Digital Twins. This hub facilitates a **holistic building life cycle management** by coordinating roles (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and their interactions with the Digital Twin. The framework’s **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** allow for **custom service integration**, enabling dynamic evolution of the system’s capabilities. While the document does not explicitly detail **microservices-inspired design** or **dynamic resource allocation**, the emphasis on **runtime configuration management** and **low-overhead updates** implies a **flexible, role-based orchestration** where the central hub manages data flow, service deployment, and operational adjustments without requiring full redeployment."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in this model-driven framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, encompassing the following phases:
  1. **Device Modeling & Configuration**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers within the BIM environment, addressing BIM’s limitations in representing Building Control Systems (BCS) components. This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Enrichment & Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models, enabling **automated generation of deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention.
  3. **DT Deployment & Synchronization**: The framework supports **real-time data exchange** between physical buildings and their digital twins, ensuring continuous synchronization. This phase includes **immersive visualization interfaces** for monitoring and control, tailored for Facility Managers.
  4. **Runtime Operation & Extension**: The DT operates dynamically, with a **modular architecture** featuring standardized extension points for integrating **custom services and analytics**. DT Engineers extend functionality to adapt to evolving operational requirements.
  **Transition Management**: The framework ensures seamless transitions between phases via **automated artifact generation** (e.g., from BIM to deployable DT components) and **modular integration points** for runtime extensions. **Lifecycle governance** is implicit in the role-based access (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and the framework’s adherence to a **complete life cycle** (from modeling to operation)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT system emphasizes **model fidelity** through the integration of **BIM-based design-time models** with **runtime operational data**, ensuring alignment between digital representations and physical asset states. Validation and verification methods are implied via the **model-driven automation framework**, which facilitates reproducible DT deployments and operational control. Uncertainty sources (e.g., sensor inaccuracies, model approximations) are not explicitly quantified, but the system’s reliance on **real-time synchronization** (via Eclipse Hono/Ditto) and **semantic integration** suggests mechanisms for error detection and correction. Quality assurance processes are inferred from the **Design Science Research (DSR) methodology**, which includes prototyping and experimentation phases to validate the DT’s operational effectiveness in AECO contexts. However, specific validation protocols (e.g., statistical methods, benchmarking) are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework relies on a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points** to support integration across heterogeneous systems. While the document references **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** for enabling real-time data exchange, no specific details are provided about the **technical network infrastructure**, including communication protocols, network topology, bandwidth/latency requirements, or security protocols (e.g., TLS/SSL, IPSec). The system emphasizes **interoperability** and **scalability**, but lacks explicit technical specifications for network reliability, redundancy, or edge/cloud connectivity."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is deployed using a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling **runtime configuration management** for adaptability without full redeployment. The framework supports **scalability** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems and ensures **maintainability** through low-overhead updates. While the document does not specify hosting platforms (e.g., cloud/edge/on-premise), computational/storage requirements, or deployment models (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes), the **modular design** and **runtime flexibility** align with scalable, adaptable deployment strategies. Availability and reliability are addressed through the system’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, though no explicit technologies or hardware are mentioned."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **BIM tools** (e.g., for device modeling and initial deployment by Building Control Engineers) and **Building Automation Systems** (for real-time monitoring/control by Facility Managers). The **DT Framework acts as a central hub** in a **hub-and-spoke model**, enabling bidirectional data exchange between physical building systems and their digital representations. While the document does not specify **interoperability protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or **enterprise system dependencies** (e.g., ERP, CMMS), it highlights **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** as a non-functional requirement (**NF_X**). The framework’s **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** support **custom service integration**, suggesting a **protocol-agnostic** approach to external system connectivity. However, no explicit details on **API gateways**, **data transformation layers**, or **third-party system dependencies** are provided in the context."
]
